function solve(chunks, queries) {
    const s = chunks.join('');
    const N = s.length;
    const wordCounts = new Map();

    function isLetter(char) {

            // Skip all consecutive non-starting characters.
            i++;

                const is_valid_hyphen = isLetter(prev_char) && isLetter(next_char);

                if (is_valid_hyphen) {
                    j++;
                } else {

                    break;
                }
            } else {

        }

        const word = s.substring(i, j);

        wordCounts.set(word, (wordCounts.get(word) || 0) + 1);

        // Move index i to start searching after the current word
        i = j;
    }

    // Process queries
    const results = [];
    for (const query of queries) {
        results.push(wordCounts.get(query) || 0);
    }

    return results;
}

### Complexity Analysis
$N_{total} \le 10^5$ (Length of $s$).
$Q_{total} \le 10^5$ (Total length of queries).

1. Building $s$: $O(N_{total})$.
2. Parsing $s$: We iterate through $s$ once. $O(N_{total})$. We generate a set of words. Let $W$ be the number of words. $\sum |word| \le N_{total}$.
3. Counting: Storing counts in a hash map is $O(\sum |word|)$.
4. Answering queries: For each query, we perform a map lookup, $O(|query|)$ time if we consider hashing/comparison complexity, but practically $O(1)$ average if the string is used as a key. Total time $O(Q_{total})$.

Overall complexity: $O(N_{total} + Q_{total})$. This is efficient enough.

```javascript
function solve(chunks, queries) {
    const s = chunks.join('');
    const N = s.length;
    const wordCounts = new Map();

    function isLetter(char) {
        return char >= 'a' && char <= 'z';
    }

    let i = 0;
    while (i < N) {
        const char = s[i];

        if (!isLetter(char)) {
            // If it's not a letter, it must be a separator (or an invalid character we just skipped).
            // Skip all consecutive non-starting characters.
            i++;
            continue;
        }

        // Potential word starts at i
        let j = i;
        while (j < N) {
            const current_char = s[j];

            if (isLetter(current_char)) {
                j++;
            } else if (current_char === '-') {
                // Check hyphen validity: must be surrounded by letters in s
                const prev_char = s[j - 1];
                const next_char = s[j + 1];

                const is_valid_hyphen = isLetter(prev_char) && isLetter(next_char);

                if (is_valid_hyphen) {
                    j++;
                } else {
                    // Invalid hyphen acts as a separator. Word ends before j.
                    break;
                }
            } else {
                // Non-letter, non-hyphen separator. Word ends before j.
                break;
            }
        }

        // Word found: s.substring(i, j)
        const word = s.substring(i, j);
        
        // Record count
        wordCounts.set(word, (wordCounts.get(word) || 0) + 1);

        // Move index i to start searching after the current word
        i = j;
    }

    // Process queries
    const results = [];
    for (const query of queries) {
        results.push(wordCounts.get(query) || 0);
    }

    return results;
}
```
